description:
Delete the workarounds that exist because of the old single-FnTypes-per-fn model. Verify the heap-safe-captures cascade still terminates correctly over the expanded specialization set. Update the typer's unit tests.

## Kills

- src/ir_typer.rs ~148-151 — "leave cont slot 0 at `any`" pin (with `Descr::any()` union). Under specialization, the cont's slot 0 is the callee's specialized return Descr, naturally.
- src/ir_typer.rs ~166-168 — `closure_reachable.contains(&f.id) { continue; }` skip. Replaced in .29.3 with any-key fallback; the bare `continue` is now dead.
- Any lingering callsite of `final_return_kinds`-style infrastructure (the .27.5.4.1 work that was reverted and never landed).

## Verify

- The heap-safe-captures cascade at src/ir_codegen.rs ~1112-1187 still terminates. Inputs are now per-specialization Descrs (tighter); the cascade should fire less aggressively, but still soundly downgrade conts with heap-typed captures. Add a test if natural coverage doesn't exercise it.

## Tests

- 13 unit tests in src/ir_typer.rs that index `mt[fn_idx].vars` need updating to either reference the any-key specialization explicitly or use a test helper.

## Acceptance

- The two workaround sites are gone (verified by grep).
- The heap-safe cascade has at least one test exercising it under per-specialization typing.
- All typer unit tests pass against the new keyed structure.
- Full fixture matrix green.
- `cargo build --release` clean (warnings-as-errors).

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.3.
title: .29.4: Kill workarounds, update tests
